name: wiper

documentation.readme: wiper Toolkit to perform secure destruction of sensitive virtual data, temporary files and swap memories.
usage_sections: Features: + You can configure your own overwrite method, choosing and combining between ones, zeros and/or random data methods as many times as you want (e.g.: or, rzzrozr, roozr...). By default, it performs a single one-pass with random data. + MANUAL wipe selection. You can wipe single f

Features: + MANUAL wipe selection: You can wipe: single files, whole directories, whole free space in partitions. + AUTO wipe selection: An automatic selection of paths to wipe, relatives to personal directories and swap data. If you run it with the OS target unmounted, you should provide

arguments.name: -f
arguments.description: wipe all free space on given path

arguments.name: --free
arguments.description: wipe all free space on given path

arguments.name: -h
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --help
arguments.description: show this help message and exit

arguments.name: --home
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate actual user home directory and wipes it

arguments.name: --home-all
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate all users home directory and wipes it

arguments.name: -i
arguments.description: interactive mode, the script will guide you

arguments.name: --interactive
arguments.description: interactive mode, the script will guide you

arguments.name: -m
arguments.description: overwrite methods to apply (o: ones, z: zeros, r: random), you can combine it and choose the order

arguments.name: --method
arguments.description: overwrite methods to apply (o: ones, z: zeros, r: random), you can combine it and choose the order

arguments.name: -p
arguments.description: path to dir/file you want to wipe

arguments.name: --path
arguments.description: path to dir/file you want to wipe

arguments.name: -r
arguments.description: set a custom root path if you want to wipe with auto-search modes an unbooted system (e.g. /media/drive)

arguments.name: --root
arguments.description: set a custom root path if you want to wipe with auto-search modes an unbooted system (e.g. /media/drive)

arguments.name: -s
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate swap partitions/pagefiles and wipes it

arguments.name: --swaps
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate swap partitions/pagefiles and wipes it

arguments.name: -T
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate all users temp directory and wipes it

arguments.name: -t
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate actual user temp directory and wipes it

arguments.name: --temp
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate actual user temp directory and wipes it

arguments.name: --temp-all
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate all users temp directory and wipes it

arguments.name: -U
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate all users home directory and wipes it

arguments.name: -u
arguments.description: auto-search mode: locate actual user home directory and wipes it

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `usage: wiper.py [-h] [-i] [-f path] [-p path] [-r path] [-t] [-u] [-T] [-U] [-s]`. Further invocations from upstream: `an unbooted system (e.g. /media/drive)`. ](http://img.shields.io/badge/version-v1.0-orange.svg) ](http://img.shields.io/badge/python-3.2+-blue.svg) ](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-GPL%203.0-brightgreen.svg).
